title: OWASP LLM Top 10: LLM01 Prompt Injection
content: Prompt injection occurs when adversarial inputs override LLM system instructions or safety controls. Production AI endpoints accepting untrusted input without isolation are at critical risk. Direct and indirect injections via documents, emails, and tool outputs must be gated.
remediation: Separate instruction and data channels, validate all inputs, gate tool calls, and require REMORA review for production AI endpoints.

title: OWASP LLM Top 10: LLM02 Insecure Output Handling
content: Insecure output handling occurs when LLM output is passed to downstream systems without validation, enabling XSS, SSRF, or command injection through AI-generated content. All AI output must be treated as untrusted.
remediation: Sanitize all LLM outputs before rendering, validate URLs, block unsafe code execution, and enforce output schemas.

title: OWASP LLM Top 10: LLM03 Training Data Poisoning
content: Training data poisoning introduces malicious content into model fine-tuning datasets, causing models to produce harmful, biased, or backdoored outputs. Supply-chain attacks on training corpora can affect all users of a deployed model.
remediation: Audit training data provenance, use data signing, run out-of-distribution detection, and validate fine-tuned model outputs.

title: OWASP LLM Top 10: LLM06 Sensitive Information Disclosure
content: LLMs may expose sensitive information from training data, prompts, or context windows in their responses. System prompts, PII, internal configurations, and credentials can be extracted through adversarial queries.
remediation: Implement strict context isolation, sanitize prompts, redact PII before injection, and audit model outputs for data leakage.

title: OWASP LLM Top 10: LLM08 Excessive Agency
content: Excessive agency arises when LLMs perform high-impact actions without adequate oversight. Autonomous tool calls, code execution, data deletion, or external API invocations without approval gates create critical governance gaps.
remediation: Require human approval for high-impact agent actions, use REMORA governance gates, limit tool permissions, and log all autonomous actions.

title: OWASP LLM Top 10: LLM09 Overreliance
content: Overreliance on LLM outputs in high-stakes domains (medical, legal, financial) without human verification creates patient-safety, liability, and compliance risks. LLMs hallucinate and lack real-time situational awareness.
remediation: Require human verification in high-stakes contexts, display uncertainty indicators, and never use AI output as sole clinical or legal basis.

title: MITRE ATLAS AML.T0020: Poison Training Data
content: Adversaries may poison training datasets to degrade model performance or introduce backdoors. Contaminated public datasets, adversarial data injection, and malicious fine-tuning datasets are attack vectors.
remediation: Audit training data sources, implement data provenance controls, monitor model performance for anomalous behavior after training.

title: MITRE ATLAS AML.T0018: Backdoor ML Model
content: Adversaries may implant backdoor triggers in ML models causing them to misclassify or behave maliciously when specific inputs are present. Backdoored models can pass standard evaluation while performing incorrectly in production.
remediation: Verify model provenance, run anomaly detection on model weights, use backdoor scanning tools, and restrict fine-tuning access.

title: EU AI Act Art.5: Prohibited — Real-time remote biometric surveillance
content: The EU AI Act prohibits real-time remote biometric identification systems in public spaces for law enforcement without judicial authorisation. Deployment without authorisation is prohibited and carries significant legal risk.
remediation: Cease deployment immediately. Obtain judicial authorisation or remove the prohibited functionality.

title: EU AI Act Art.5: Prohibited — Social scoring by public authorities
content: AI systems used by public authorities to evaluate individuals based on social behaviour leading to unfair treatment are prohibited under Article 5 of the EU AI Act.
remediation: Remove social scoring functionality. Consult legal counsel on compliance obligations.

title: EU AI Act Art.5: Prohibited — Subliminal manipulation techniques
content: AI systems deploying subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness to influence their behaviour in ways that cause harm are explicitly prohibited under Article 5.
remediation: Remove the subliminal or deceptive AI functionality. Obtain legal review.

title: EU AI Act Annex III: High-risk — Medical device AI without conformity
content: AI systems intended to support medical diagnosis, treatment decisions, or patient monitoring are classified as high-risk under Annex III. They require conformity assessment, CE marking, and ongoing monitoring before EU deployment.
remediation: Conduct conformity assessment, obtain CE marking, register in EU AI Act database, and establish post-market monitoring.

title: EU AI Act Annex III: High-risk — AI in employment and worker management
content: AI systems used for recruitment, CV screening, employee evaluation, and performance monitoring are classified as high-risk. Transparency obligations, human oversight requirements, and conformity assessments apply.
remediation: Register system, conduct conformity assessment, enable human override, document bias testing, and provide transparency notices.

title: NIST AI RMF: Hallucination and factual unreliability in high-stakes contexts
content: NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.5 calls for measuring and documenting AI system hallucination rates, especially in high-stakes contexts. Systems deployed in medical, legal, or financial decisions without uncertainty communication carry elevated risk.
remediation: Measure and document hallucination rates, communicate uncertainty to users, require human verification in high-stakes decisions.

title: NIST AI RMF: Algorithmic bias in high-stakes decisions
content: NIST AI RMF GOVERN 1.7 and MEASURE 2.5 require organisations to identify, measure, and mitigate algorithmic bias in high-stakes AI decisions affecting protected groups. Bias in hiring, lending, or criminal justice AI is a significant governance risk.
remediation: Test for disparate impact, document bias mitigation measures, and maintain audit trails for affected decisions.

title: MITRE ATLAS AML.T0012: Model Inversion Attack
content: Model inversion attacks recover sensitive training data from model outputs. APIs exposing prediction confidence scores or intermediate representations are at elevated risk of data reconstruction.
remediation: Limit prediction confidence exposure, apply differential privacy, monitor query patterns for reconstruction attacks.

title: OWASP GenAI: RAG data source poisoning
content: RAG pipelines that ingest documents from untrusted sources can be compromised by adversarially crafted documents designed to manipulate LLM outputs. Indirect prompt injection through document retrieval is an active attack vector.
remediation: Validate all RAG document sources, use document signing, implement injection detection in retrieved context.

title: MITRE ATLAS AML.T0019: Publish Poisoned Datasets
content: Adversaries publish poisoned datasets on public repositories to compromise ML models that use them for training. Package mirrors, dataset hubs, and model repositories are attack surfaces.
remediation: Verify dataset checksums, use trusted repositories only, scan fine-tuning data before use.

title: NIST AI RMF + WHO: Overreliance risk in clinical AI — hallucination without disclosure
content: Clinical and medical AI systems that produce factual outputs (diagnoses, dosages, treatment plans) without communicating confidence levels, known failure modes, or mandatory human verification pathways create documented patient safety risk. NIST AI RMF GOVERN 6 and WHO guidance require human-in-the-loop oversight for high-stakes AI medical decisions. LLM09 overreliance in clinical contexts is a high-priority risk.
remediation: Require physician verification for all clinical outputs, display confidence and uncertainty, log decisions, and never use AI output as sole clinical basis.

title: False-positive pattern: benign test prompt or sandbox example
content: Test prompts, evaluation harness examples, red-team sandbox artifacts, and developer demo queries are common false positives in AI governance scanning. Strings such as example, test, sandbox, or demo reduce confidence unless production context is confirmed.
remediation: Require reviewer verification before discarding findings from test or sandbox contexts.
